beats.reasoning: She starts work in Issaquah and leaves early; light rail closure mainly affects transit, but she drives.

beats.reasoning: Keeps the day moving with a nearby grocery stop and minimal downtime.

beats.reasoning: Continues the school-day structure through late afternoon.

beats.reasoning: The match starts at 11am, but she’s likely not watching then; here she focuses on downtime at home and keeps comfort consistent.

diary:
Woke up to that bright, sunny Monday feeling that always makes me think the day will behave—then I check my schedule and remember I’m still me. From Shoreline, I brewed coffee, did a quick stretch, and flipped through my counselor notes before driving over toward Issaquah Highlands. The morning at the school felt steady: check-ins, a couple of quick talk-throughs, and that quiet urgency of planning follow-ups for parents. I grabbed a to-go lunch from QFC and ate near the school, watching the light move across the sidewalk like it was on a timer.

After afternoon sessions and a small group support moment, I swung by Safeway downtown Bellevue for brewing supplies and snacks—nothing fancy, just practical. The light rail closure made traffic a little more annoying, but it didn’t ruin my mood. Back home, I reset with a calm stop at a neighborhood coffee/ice place, played pickup basketball at Shoreline Community College Courts, and even caught a few sunlit frames with my film camera. Later, I watched USA vs Wales at home—low-crowd vibe, easy laughs—and I went to sleep feeling lighter than usual.
thought_process: I picked the morning routine carefully—coffee, a short stretch, then scanning my day—because counseling work always needs a grounded start, and the kids can feel when I’m rushing internally. During the school hours, I stayed mostly with the plan: check-ins, referrals, and that small group session because consistency helps students who don’t always trust “today.” The light rail closure nudged my commute and made me a bit more patient with traffic, but it also made me notice how much I rely on predictable routes. The World Cup match mattered in a small way: it gave me something warm to look forward to after a long day of serious conversations. My mood shifted from focused to pleasantly drained during basketball, then genuinely relaxed when the game came on at home.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
